tasks.title: Extract validatePipelineField helper in tasks.js
tasks.description: Create a standalone validatePipelineField(value) function in src/handlers/tasks.js that validates the pipeline field consistently across all call sites. Returns { valid, data, error }. An empty array returns { valid: true, data: undefined } (treated as absent). Maximum 20 elements, each a non-empty string ≤ 50 chars.
acceptance_criteria: validatePipelineField(undefined) returns { valid: true, data: undefined }
validatePipelineField([]) returns { valid: true, data: undefined }
validatePipelineField(['a','b']) returns { valid: true, data: ['a','b'] }
validatePipelineField('string') returns { valid: false, error: '...' }
validatePipelineField(new Array(21).fill('x')) returns { valid: false, error: '...' }
validatePipelineField(['', 'agent']) returns { valid: false, error: '...' }
validatePipelineField(['agent']) trims each element
Unit tests cover all branches above

tasks.title: Extend handleCreateTask to accept and store pipeline field
tasks.description: In src/handlers/tasks.js, call validatePipelineField on body.pipeline inside handleCreateTask. Store the validated array on the task object when non-empty. Omit the field entirely (do not store undefined or null) when absent or empty.
acceptance_criteria: POST /spaces/:id/tasks with pipeline: ['developer-agent'] stores the field on the task in the JSON file
POST /spaces/:id/tasks without pipeline creates a task with no pipeline key in JSON
POST /spaces/:id/tasks with pipeline: [] creates a task with no pipeline key in JSON
POST /spaces/:id/tasks with pipeline: 'string' returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR
POST /spaces/:id/tasks with pipeline: [new Array(21)] returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR
GET /tasks response includes pipeline field when set on a task
Existing tasks without pipeline field are unaffected

tasks.title: Extend handleUpdateTask to accept and store pipeline field
tasks.description: In src/handlers/tasks.js, add 'pipeline' to UPDATABLE_FIELDS. Call validatePipelineField in the validation block. Apply the change: non-empty validated array replaces the field; empty array (data: undefined) deletes the key from the task object. Identical pattern to how 'description' and 'assigned' are cleared.
acceptance_criteria: PUT /tasks/:id with { pipeline: ['senior-architect'] } updates the field and returns it in the response
PUT /tasks/:id with { pipeline: [] } clears the field (key absent from returned task and from JSON file)
PUT /tasks/:id with { pipeline: 'bad' } returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR
PUT /tasks/:id with no pipeline key leaves the existing pipeline field unchanged
PUT /tasks/:id with { title: 'x' } still works (pipeline not required)
updatedAt is updated on every successful pipeline change
Unit tests cover set, clear, and leave-unchanged paths

tasks.title: Extend handleCreateRun to resolve task.pipeline in the fallback chain
tasks.description: In src/handlers/pipeline.js handleCreateRun, after the TASK_NOT_FOUND and TASK_NOT_IN_TODO guards (which already read the task), extract task.pipeline. Insert it between the explicit stages param and space.pipeline in the resolution chain. Log a structured event { event: 'run.pipeline_resolved', resolvedFrom: 'task|space|default', stages } to stderr.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/runs with no stages and a task that has pipeline: ['developer-agent'] uses developer-agent as the only stage
POST /api/v1/runs with no stages and a task without pipeline falls back to space.pipeline if set
POST /api/v1/runs with no stages, no task.pipeline, no space.pipeline uses DEFAULT_STAGES
POST /api/v1/runs with explicit stages always uses those stages regardless of task.pipeline
A structured log line with resolvedFrom is emitted to stderr on every run creation
Integration test: end-to-end run creation with each resolution path

tasks.title: Extend auto-task system prompt to emit optional pipeline field
tasks.description: Update src/prompts/autotask-system.txt to add rule 5 (emit optional pipeline array) and extend the response schema to include pipeline?: string[]. The field is optional; the AI should only include it when the task scope clearly maps to a non-default agent sequence. Known agent IDs to document: senior-architect, ux-api-designer, developer-agent, code-reviewer, qa-engineer-e2e.
acceptance_criteria: The updated system prompt is syntactically valid plain text
The schema comment in the prompt shows pipeline as optional
Known agent IDs are listed so the AI does not hallucinate IDs
The rule explicitly states 'omit when uncertain' to avoid over-production
Existing prompt rules 1–4 are unchanged

tasks.title: Extend handleAutoTaskGenerate to validate and store pipeline field
tasks.description: In src/handlers/autoTask.js, after callCLI returns rawTasks, call validatePipelineField on each task's pipeline value. Apply soft validation: if the field is present but invalid, strip it (log a warning) rather than rejecting the whole task. Strip any element whose trimmed value is not among the known agent IDs resolved from PIPELINE_AGENTS_DIR. Emit a structured log line when a pipeline field is stored.
acceptance_criteria: When AI returns pipeline: ['developer-agent', 'qa-engineer-e2e'], the stored task has that pipeline field
When AI returns pipeline: ['nonexistent-agent'], the element is stripped; task is stored with no pipeline field
When AI returns pipeline: 'string' (wrong type), the field is stripped; task is stored without it
When AI returns no pipeline field, task is stored without it (unchanged behaviour)
A structured log line { event: 'autotask.pipeline_field_set', taskId, stages } is emitted when the field is stored
Existing autotask create and confirm flows are unaffected

tasks.title: Extend MCP kanban_update_task to expose pipeline parameter
tasks.description: In mcp/mcp-server.js, add pipeline: z.array(z.string()).optional() to the kanban_update_task tool schema. In the handler, include pipeline in the fields object if defined (same pattern as title/type/description/assigned). In mcp/kanban-client.js updateTask function, forward pipeline in the request body when defined.
acceptance_criteria: kanban_update_task({ id, pipeline: ['developer-agent'], spaceId }) successfully updates the task's pipeline field
kanban_update_task({ id, pipeline: [], spaceId }) clears the task's pipeline field
kanban_update_task({ id, title: 'x', spaceId }) with no pipeline leaves pipeline unchanged
MCP tool description clearly explains the override behavior and empty-to-clear semantics
The Zod schema rejects non-string array elements (z.string() enforces this)

tasks.title: Extend frontend Task type and openPipelineConfirm resolver
tasks.description: In frontend/src/types/index.ts, add pipeline?: string[] to the Task interface. In frontend/src/stores/useAppStore.ts openPipelineConfirm, update the stage resolution to prefer the task's pipeline field (fetched from the board task in the store) before falling back to space.pipeline and then agentSettings defaults. The task object is available via board tasks in the store.
acceptance_criteria: Task interface compiles with pipeline?: string[] field — no TypeScript errors
When a task has pipeline: ['developer-agent', 'qa-engineer-e2e'] and openPipelineConfirm is called, the modal opens with those two stages pre-populated
When the task has no pipeline field, the modal falls back to space.pipeline (existing behavior)
When the task has pipeline: [] the modal falls back to space.pipeline (empty treated as absent)
No regression in existing openPipelineConfirm behavior for tasks without the field
TypeScript strict mode: no implicit any or type assertions added

tasks.title: Add pipeline field editor to TaskDetailPanel
tasks.description: Extend frontend/src/components/board/TaskDetailPanel.tsx to display and edit the task's pipeline field. Collapsed state: shows 'Pipeline: (space default)' in text-text-secondary when absent, or a compact pill chain 'agent-a → agent-b' in text-text-primary when set, with Edit and Clear buttons. Edit state: ordered list with up/down arrows and remove buttons (identical pattern to PipelineConfirmModal stage list), plus a dropdown to add agents from availableAgents. Explicit Save/Cancel buttons. On save, calls store.updateTask(taskId, { pipeline }). Uses design system tokens only — no inline styles.
acceptance_criteria: Task with no pipeline shows 'Pipeline: (space default)' in the detail panel
Task with pipeline shows the agent chain as text with → separators
Clicking Edit opens the ordered list with current stages pre-populated
User can add, remove, and reorder stages; clicking Save calls updateTask with the new array
Clicking Cancel restores the previous value without saving
Clicking Clear (when pipeline is set) calls updateTask with pipeline: [] and UI reverts to 'space default' label
The editor only shows agents present in availableAgents (consistent with PipelineConfirmModal)
No inline style attributes — Tailwind tokens only
Component passes TypeScript strict mode compilation
Existing TaskDetailPanel functionality (title, type, assigned, description) is unaffected

tasks.title: Write backend unit and integration tests for pipeline field
tasks.description: Add test coverage for the pipeline field across tasks.js (create/update), pipeline.js (resolution chain), and autoTask.js (AI output parsing). Use the existing Node.js test runner (node:test). Cover: valid create with pipeline, valid update, clear via empty array, invalid type rejection, resolution chain priority order, auto-task soft validation.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes with zero failures
Coverage includes: T-001 validatePipelineField all branches, T-002 create paths, T-003 update and clear paths, T-004 all three resolution-chain outcomes, T-006 soft-validation strip paths
No existing tests are broken

tasks.title: Write frontend unit tests for pipeline field UI
tasks.description: Add Vitest + React Testing Library tests for the pipeline field section in TaskDetailPanel. Test: collapsed state (no pipeline), collapsed state (with pipeline), edit mode open/close, save calls updateTask with correct payload, clear calls updateTask with empty array, cancel restores state.
acceptance_criteria: cd frontend && npm test passes with zero failures
Tests cover collapsed (absent), collapsed (present), edit, save, clear, cancel
No existing frontend tests broken
